pi_items.source: Task description line: 'Create a folder named "Triage-Critical"'; Matches verifier.py line 62 which looks for folder '.Triage-Critical'
Task description line: 'Forward the same email to "escalation@techcorp.com"'; Matches verifier.py line 44 and 121 which checks for ESCALATION_EMAIL in recipient address

Direct inspection of /workspace/assets/emails/ham/*.eml files using grep -lE 'panic|fatal|fail|error'; Found: 5 total files matching (0 with 'panic', 0 with 'fatal', 4 with 'fail', 3 with 'error'). Matches setup_task.sh line 35 and verifier.py line 43

Task description requires 'move at least 3 of these critical emails'; Verified 5 emails available with critical keywords (exceeds minimum of 3); Matches verifier.py line 77 which requires triage_count >= 3

privileged_info_summary: The critical_issue_triage_and_escalation task uses the SpamAssassin public corpus as its email dataset, not synthetic data. The task requires identifying emails containing the keywords 'panic', 'fatal', 'fail', or 'error' - of which 5 emails in the dataset contain at least one of these keywords (4 with 'fail', 3 with 'error', 0 with 'panic' or 'fatal'). The minimum requirement is 3 critical emails, which is easily satisfied. The triage folder name 'Triage-Critical' and escalation recipient email 'escalation@techcorp.com' are both correctly specified in the metadata and match the task description requirements. All metadata values are verified against both the email corpus and the verifier logic.
